mnemonics.title: Key Metrics: B.D.N.L.
mnemonics.content: Bandwidth, Diameter, Node degree, Latency. These four metrics define network quality. Bisection bandwidth measures worst-case throughput, diameter measures worst-case latency, node degree measures wiring complexity.

mnemonics.title: Hypercube Formulas
mnemonics.content: For N = 2^d nodes: Diameter = d = log2(N), Node degree = d = log2(N), Total links = N*d/2. Think: hypercube dimension d controls everything.

mnemonics.title: MIN Stages Formula
mnemonics.content: Multistage Interconnection Network: Stages = log2(N), Switches per stage = N/2. For an 8-input omega: 3 stages, 4 switches each. Think: log2 is the magic number for multistage networks.

mnemonics.title: Static vs Dynamic: F.R.
mnemonics.content: Static = Fixed wiring (Ring, Mesh, Hypercube -- cannot change connections). Dynamic = Reconfigurable switching (Crossbar, Omega -- paths change based on routing). Think: Static is set in stone, Dynamic is dial-a-connection.

mnemonics.title: Topology Cost Ordering
mnemonics.content: Bus < Ring < Mesh < Hypercube < Crossbar in cost. Bus is cheapest (one wire), Crossbar is most expensive (N^2 switches). Performance follows the reverse order: Crossbar > Hypercube > Mesh > Ring > Bus.
